back_translation: Israel set out with all that he had, and he came to Beersheba, and he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
God spoke to Israel in a vision of the night, saying, 'Jacob, Jacob!' And he answered, 'Here I am.'
He said, 'I am God, the God of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation.'
I will go down with you to Egypt, and I will surely bring you back. And Joseph will close your eyes with his hand.'
Then Jacob departed from Beersheba, and the sons of Israel carried Jacob their father, their little ones, and their wives in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
They took the livestock and the possessions they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and Jacob and all his offspring came to Egypt.
Jacob brought with him to Egypt his sons, his grandsons, his daughters, his granddaughters, and all his offspring.
These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt — Jacob and his sons. The firstborn of Jacob: Reuben.
The sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. But Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan. The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.
The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puvah, Job, and Shimron.
The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
These are the sons of Leah whom she bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram, along with Dinah his daughter. The total number of sons and daughters: thirty-three.
The sons of Gad: Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and Serah their sister. The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel.
These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Leah; she bore these to Jacob — sixteen persons.
The sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.
Manasseh and Ephraim were born to Joseph in the land of Egypt, by Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, priest of On.
The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
These are the sons of Rachel born to Jacob — fourteen in all.
The son of Dan: Hushim.
The sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Rachel; she bore these to Jacob — seven in all.
All those who came with Jacob to Egypt — those coming out of his loins, besides the wives of Jacob's sons — totaled sixty-six persons.
The sons of Joseph born to him in Egypt: two persons. All the persons of the house of Jacob who came to Egypt: seventy.
Jacob sent Judah ahead of him to Joseph, to lead him to Goshen. When they came to the land of Goshen,
Joseph harnessed his chariot and went up to meet Israel his father at Goshen. When he appeared to him, he fell upon his neck and wept upon his neck for a long time.
Israel said to Joseph, 'Now I can die, after I have seen your face, that you are still alive.'
Joseph said to his brothers and to his father's household, 'I will go up and tell Pharaoh, saying, 'My brothers and my father's household, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
These men are shepherds of flocks, for they have been keepers of livestock. They have brought their flocks and their cattle and all that they own.'
When Pharaoh calls you and asks, 'What is your occupation?'

you shall say, 'Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth until now — both we and our fathers,' so that you may dwell in the land of Goshen, for every shepherd of flock is an abomination to Egypt.'
